subject: [PATCH 2/3] mm/swap: use swap_ops to register swap device's methods

reviews.author: Baoquan He
reviews.summary: Reviewer Baoquan He noted that the current implementation assumes a swap backend always has a writeback operation, but this may not be true in all cases; he suggested adding a warning at the beginning of zswap_writeback_entry() to handle potential future scenarios where a swap backend lacks a read/write_folio operation.
reviews.raw_body:
Good point. Currently, there's no case where a swap backend without
write_folio. While in the future there could be, assume you have noticed
the swap tier patchset introduced by Youngjun.

Here, the si->ops checking is purely defensive programming. I can add a
warn at the beginnin of zswap_writeback_entry(). If there's any case
involving no read/write_folio, adaption can be made at that time.

2026-03-03.patch_summary: This patch modifies the zswap writeback behavior to check if the underlying swap device has a writeback operation before proceeding. It adds a check at the beginning of the zswap_writeback_entry function to bail out early if no writeback operation is available, potentially adding a warning for such cases.

2026-03-02.patch_summary: This patch modifies the zswap writeback behavior to check if the underlying swap device has a writeback operation before proceeding. It adds a check at the beginning of the zswap_writeback_entry function to bail out early if no writeback operation is available, potentially adding a warning for such cases.
